mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate above 5.0% signals weakening labor market conditions that depress corporate earnings expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite rising UNRATE.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in GDP and housing starts indicates strong aggregate demand, which drives corporate revenue growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 60% of instances where both GDPC1 and HOUST show positive growth.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in industrial production signals robust supply-side capacity utilization, which supports corporate profit margins and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 60% of instances where INDPRO shows positive growth.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI reading above 210.565 signals persistent inflationary pressures that prompt central bank tightening, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite CPI above threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth above 138,495 thousand persons indicates a tightening labor market that boosts consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 60% of instances where PAYEMS shows positive growth.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in retail sales and housing starts reflect strong consumer and business demand, which drive equity valuations higher and generate positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 60% of instances where both RSAFS and HOUST show positive growth.

mechanism_narrative: A monetary tightening cycle characterized by rising federal funds rates and inflation expectations leads to higher discount rates and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where both FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL show upward trends.
